- Materials: Welcome kit with backpack, cap, and activity notebook

### Practical comparisons
- Cost: 250 CHF per week (vs. 300 CHF for summer camps in Lugano)
- Distance: 30 minutes from Lugano (vs. 1 hour for camps in Milan)
- Capacity: 25 children per week (vs. 30-40 in other facilities)

### Open day and information
For more information on the IngleseBimbi® method, you can participate in the Instagram LIVE of the IngleseBimbi profile on Wednesday, May 6, at 2 PM. Among the proposed experiences, there is also the possibility to visit the stables, paddocks, and pick ingredients directly from the vegetable garden for the snack. Meals can be prepared on the farm and consumed outdoors, under a gazebo. Two open days are planned, on May 9 and 23, to visit the facility and meet the organizers.

> "The goal is to create a safe and stimulating environment where children can learn while playing" - Domitilla Lenzi

For information and bookings, you can contact the estate via WhatsApp at 351 551 1852 or write to info@tenutalanovella.it. Accepted payments: bank transfer, PayPal, credit card. ### Practical Implications for Parents
The Happy Farm Summer Camp, located in Malnate, represents a unique opportunity for parents seeking an educational and fun experience for their children. The combination of outdoor activities, contact with nature, and English learning offers a stimulating and safe environment. Parents can be certain that their children will be engaged in activities that promote creativity, curiosity, and respect for the environment. The summer camp welcomes children aged 5 to 11 and offers a 1:5 ratio between educators and participants, ensuring personalized attention. Costs range from 200 to 250 CHF per week, depending on the package chosen, and include meals, teaching materials, and insurance.

### Comparison with Other Summer Offers
Compared to other summer camps, the Happy Farm Summer Camp stands out for its focus on nature and farm life. The presence of animals such as rabbits, goats, and chickens, along with the opportunity to participate in practical workshops, offers a more authentic and engaging experience. Additionally, the introduction of English through the IngleseBimbi® method adds value, allowing children to learn the language naturally and playfully. Unlike other summer camps that focus primarily on sports or artistic activities, Happy Farm integrates language learning with practical farm experience.

### Scenarios and Opportunities
Parents can take advantage of this opportunity to give their children an unforgettable summer, combining fun and learning. The estate offers a safe and welcoming environment, with qualified staff taking care of the children. Additionally, the possibility of participating in open days allows visitors to tour the facility and meet the organizers, facilitating the choice and trust in the summer camp.

#### Operational Checklist
- **Registration**: Complete the online form by June 15 to take advantage of the 10% early bird discount.
- Documents: Present the medical certificate and consent form for outdoor activities.
- Payment: Make the payment by July 1 to confirm the booking.
- Equipment: Bring comfortable clothing, sneakers, and a change of clothes.

#### Regulations and Boundaries
For parents working in Ticino, it is important to consider the regulations on cross-border work. According to the bilateral agreement between Switzerland and the EU, cross-border workers can benefit from tax advantages, but they must meet residence and work requirements. For example, cross-border workers residing in Italy and working in Switzerland only pay taxes in their country of residence but must file a tax return in Switzerland. Additionally, it is important to verify the specific regulations for summer camps, such as the obligation of health insurance and compliance with workplace safety regulations.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A parent working in Lugano and residing in Varese can enroll their child in the summer camp in Malnate without any issues, benefiting from tax advantages for cross-border workers.
- Scenario 2: A parent working in Bellinzona and residing in Como must verify the specific regulations for cross-border workers but can still enroll their child in the summer camp, taking advantage of the educational and fun activities offered.

> "The Happy Farm Summer Camp is a unique opportunity for children to learn English naturally and enjoyably, combining farm experience with outdoor activities."

In conclusion, the Happy Farm Summer Camp offers a unique experience that combines nature, language learning, and fun, representing a valuable opportunity for parents seeking an educational and engaging alternative for their children.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate
To participate in the Happy Farm Summer Camp, parents can follow these steps:
1. Contact the estate via WhatsApp at +39 351 551 1852 or email info@tenutalanovella.it. The estate is located in Malnate, just a few kilometers from the border with Ticino, in an area easily accessible from towns like Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Lugano.
2. Attend the open days on May 9 and 23, from 14:00 to 17:00, to visit the facility and meet the organizers. During the open days, parents will also have the opportunity to meet some of the tutors who will accompany the children during the summer camp.
3. Fill out the participation form available on the estate's website, specifying any allergies, dietary needs, or other relevant information for the safety of the children.
4. Pay the participation fee of 450 CHF per week, which includes meals, accommodation, educational activities, and materials. It is possible to pay in monthly installments, with a 30% deposit at the time of registration and the balance due by June 15.

### Costs and Details
Costs and specific details are now available on the estate's website. The summer camp is open to children aged 6 to 12 and offers a weekly program from Monday to Friday, from 9:00 to 17:00. For parents working in Switzerland, it is possible to use the pre and post-camp service, from 8:00 to 9:00 and from 17:00 to 18:00, for an additional cost of 50 CHF per week.

📊 Cost Comparison:
- Happy Farm Summer Camp: 450 CHF per week
- Summer Camps in Ticino: on average 500-600 CHF per week
- Summer Camps in Italy: on average 300-400 EUR per week

### Useful Tools
For further information and to plan the participation in the summer camp, parents can use the [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to estimate costs and organize the family budget, taking into account transportation, meals, and accommodation expenses.

💡 Operational Checklist:
- [ ] Contact the estate for information
- [ ] Attend the open days
- [ ] Fill out the participation form
- [ ] Pay the participation fee
- [ ] Provide medical documentation and authorizations

### Conclusion
The Happy Farm Summer Camp at Tenuta La Novella represents a unique opportunity for children to spend a summer focused on nature, farm life, and learning English. Parents can be assured that their children will be in a safe and stimulating environment, with activities that promote creativity and respect for the environment. For further information and to book, contact the estate via WhatsApp or email.

⚠️ Regulations:
- Health Insurance: Children must be covered by a valid health insurance in Italy. Parents must provide the documentation at the time of registration.
- Authorizations: Written authorization is required for any medical treatments and for leaving the camp.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/summer-camp-a-malnate-alla-tenuta-la-novella-settimane-tra-fattoria-natura-e-inglese/2560273/)*

item.title: Ticino police project abandoned by State Council
item.description: The State Council has decided not to proceed with the Ticino Police project, launched in 2016 to improve collaboration between cantonal and municipal police.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ticino Police project abandoned by the State Council
- Consultation with municipalities and institutional actors
- Opposition to proposed changes

## Key facts
- **What**: Abandonment of the Ticino Police project
- When: Decision made after consultation in 2025
- Where: Canton of Ticino
- Who: State Council
- Amount: Not specified

The Ticino Police project, launched in 2016 with the aim of improving the organization and collaboration between the Cantonal Police and municipal police forces, saw the light of day thanks to a working group that thoroughly analyzed the division of tasks, responsibilities, and governance models between the two institutional levels. The working group developed a proposal based on the principles of subsidiarity and financial neutrality, distinguishing between political and operational responsibilities and valuing the proximity role of municipal police forces.

During 2025, the report was put out for consultation with municipalities and key institutional actors. From the analysis of the observations received, opposition to the changes proposed by the joint working group emerged. The State Council took note of these observations and decided not to proceed further with the project, formally concluding the project phase.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The abandonment of the Ticino Police project could have significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in the Canton of Ticino. The State Council's decision not to proceed with the project means that there will be no immediate changes in the management of police forces at the cantonal and municipal levels. However, the work carried out in recent years constitutes an important knowledge base for potential future developments, particularly regarding the definition of roles between the Canton and municipalities and the strengthening of police force coordination mechanisms in the Canton.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the launch of the Ticino Police project, collaboration between the Cantonal Police and municipal police forces was already underway, but the project aimed to further improve this collaboration. The abandonment of the project means that the situation will remain substantially unchanged, with municipal police forces continuing to perform their proximity role and the Cantonal Police managing cantonal competence issues.

### Future scenarios

Despite the abandonment of the project, the work carried out could be used in the future to improve collaboration between police forces. The State Council emphasized that the work carried out constitutes an important knowledge base for potential future developments. This means that, although the project is not being pursued now, the ideas and proposals developed could be reused in the future to improve the management of police forces in the Canton of Ticino. ## Practical Analysis

The abandonment of the Ticino Police project raises some questions about how collaboration between the Cantonal Police and municipal police will be managed in the future. The working group had developed a proposal based on the principles of subsidiarity and financial neutrality, distinguishing between political and operational responsibilities. This approach could have led to greater efficiency and coordination among police forces.

### Implications for Security

The decision not to proceed with the project could have implications for security in the Canton of Ticino. Municipal police play a crucial role in managing local security, and their collaboration with the Cantonal Police is essential to ensure an effective response to emergencies. The abandonment of the project means that there will be no immediate changes in this area, but it could also mean that there will be no significant improvements in the management of police forces.

### Comparison with Other Regions

In other regions of Switzerland, there are examples of effective collaboration between cantonal and municipal police forces. For example, in the Canton of Zurich, collaboration between police forces is well-established and has led to significant improvements in security management. The abandonment of the Ticino Police project could mean that the Canton of Ticino will miss the opportunity to improve collaboration between police forces in a similar way.

### Future Scenarios

Despite the abandonment of the project, the work done could be used in the future to improve collaboration between police forces. The State Council has emphasized that the work done constitutes an important knowledge base for any future developments. This means that, although the project is not being pursued now, the ideas and proposals developed could be reused in the future to improve the management of police forces in the Canton of Ticino.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers living and working in the Canton of Ticino, the abandonment of the Ticino Police project may not have an immediate impact. However, it could mean that there will be no significant improvements in the management of police forces in the short term. It is important that cross-border workers continue to follow local regulations and collaborate with police forces to ensure public safety and order.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete Actions

The abandonment of the Ticino Police project does not immediately change the situation for cross-border workers in the Canton of Ticino. However, it is important to be aware of the long-term implications and possible future developments. Here are some concrete actions that cross-border workers can take:

### Inform Yourself About Local Regulations

Cross-border workers should continue to inform themselves about local regulations and safety procedures. This includes knowing the contacts of the municipal and cantonal police and knowing how to reach them in case of emergency. Information can be found on the websites of the local authorities or by directly contacting the police forces.

### Collaborate with the Police Forces

Collaboration with the police forces is essential to ensure safety and public order. Cross-border workers should collaborate with the municipal and cantonal police, providing useful information and reporting any security issues. This helps to maintain a safe environment for all residents and workers in the Canton of Ticino.

### Monitor Future Developments

Despite the abandonment of the Ticino Police project, the work done could be used in the future to improve collaboration between the police forces. Cross-border workers should monitor future developments and stay informed about any changes in regulations or safety procedures. This can be done by following local news or consulting the websites of the cantonal authorities.

### Use Useful Tools

To stay updated on regulations and safety procedures, cross-border workers can use tools such as the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) or the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare). These tools can help better understand local regulations and plan their activities in a safe and legal manner.

### Conclusion

The abandonment of the Ticino Police project does not immediately change the situation for cross-border workers in the Canton of Ticino. However, it is important to be aware of the long-term implications and possible future developments. Informing yourself about local regulations, collaborating with the police forces, and monitoring future developments are concrete actions that cross-border workers can take to ensure their own safety and that of the community.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/nessun-futuro-per-il-progetto-polizia-ticinese-432548)*

item.title: Fuel tax cut extended: diesel 24.4c, petrol 6c discount
item.description: The Italian government has extended the reduction of fuel excise duties for three weeks, with a differentiated discount for petrol and diesel.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three-week extension for the reduction of fuel excise duties
- Diesel discount: 24.4 cents per litre
- Petrol discount: 6 cents per litre

## Key facts
- **What**: Extension of fuel excise duty reduction
- When: From 1st May 2026 for three weeks
- Where: Italy
- Who: Italian government
- Amount: Diesel 24.4 cents/litre, petrol 6 cents/litre

The Italian government has extended the reduction of fuel excise duties for another three weeks, which was set to end on Friday, 1st May. The measure, introduced in April, aims to contain the rise in prices caused by the war in the Middle East. The discount for diesel remains at 24.4 cents per litre, while for petrol it drops to 6 cents per litre. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni explained that the discount on diesel has been kept higher because the increase in market price has been heavier for this fuel.

### Impact on cross-border workers
The extension of the reduction of fuel excise duties will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. With the cost of fuel representing an important part of monthly expenses, this measure can alleviate the economic burden for those who travel daily between the two countries. However, it is important to note that the reduction of the discount on petrol could influence mobility choices, pushing some to consider alternative means of transport.

### Comparison with other European measures
While Italy extends the reduction of excise duties, other European countries are introducing measures to contain fuel consumption. This differentiated approach raises questions about long-term strategies to address the energy crisis. Reducing fuel consumption could become a priority in the future, especially in light of geopolitical tensions and concerns about resource scarcity. ## Analysis of the implications
The extension of the reduction in fuel excise duties represents a temporary relief for Italian consumers, but it also raises some important questions. The decision to maintain a higher discount for diesel compared to petrol reflects market dynamics and the specific needs of different sectors. However, it is essential to consider the long-term implications of this measure.

### Market effects
The reduction in excise duties can influence the fuel market in various ways. On the one hand, it can stimulate demand, especially for diesel, which benefits from a more substantial discount. On the other hand, it could also encourage the use of more fuel-efficient vehicles, such as diesel ones, which are generally more economical to manage.

### Environmental considerations
While the reduction in excise duties can provide immediate economic relief, it is important to consider the environmental implications. Encouraging the use of fossil fuels could contradict sustainability and emission reduction goals. In a context of growing environmental awareness, this measure could be seen as a step back from energy transition policies.

### Comparison with other European policies
In contrast to the Italian approach, other European countries are adopting measures to reduce fuel consumption. For example, some states are introducing incentives for the purchase of electric or hybrid vehicles, or implementing low-emission zones in cities. These policies aim to reduce dependence on fossil fuels and promote more sustainable mobility.

### Future scenarios
It is possible that, in the future, Italy will follow the example of other European countries and introduce measures to contain fuel consumption. This could include increasing excise duties, introducing speed limits, or promoting alternative means of transport. However, for the moment, the extension of the reduction in excise duties represents a response to the current energy crisis, with the aim of easing the economic burden on consumers.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now
For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and live in Italy, it's important to be aware of the implications of the extension of the reduction in fuel excise duties. Here are some concrete steps to follow:

1. **Monitor fuel prices**: With the reduction in excise duties, fuel prices may vary. It's useful to monitor prices at service stations along your usual routes to ensure you get the best quality-price ratio.
2. Consider alternative means of transport: If the cost of fuel remains a significant burden, it might be useful to evaluate alternative means of transport, such as carpooling or using public transport. In Ticino, for example, there are several public transport options that can be convenient for cross-border workers.
3. Plan your trips: Planning your trips in advance can help reduce fuel costs. For example, avoiding peak hours can reduce travel time and, consequently, fuel consumption.
4. Use comparison tools: There are several online tools that allow you to compare fuel prices at different service stations. Using these tools can help you find the best deals and save on fuel costs.
5. Stay informed: Fuel policies can change rapidly. It's important to stay up-to-date on the latest news and any changes to excise duties or other tax measures that could affect fuel costs.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to optimize their trips and reduce fuel costs, tools like the [fuel cost calculator](nav:calculator) and the [fuel price comparator](nav:exchange) can be useful. These tools can help you plan your trips better and find the most convenient deals.

### Conclusion
The extension of the reduction in fuel excise duties represents temporary relief for Italian consumers, but it's important to consider the long-term implications. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, it's essential to monitor fuel prices, consider alternative means of transport, and stay informed about the latest news. Using comparison tools and planning trips in advance can help reduce costs and optimize mobility.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/il-governo-ha-prorogato-di-tre-settimane-la-riduzione-delle-accise-sui-carburanti/2563222/)*

item.title: Zurich court halts Sunrise's abusive practices
item.description: The Zurich district court has ruled that two Sunrise contractual clauses regarding price increases and termination procedures are illegal.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Zurich court halts Sunrise's abusive practices
- Clauses on increases and terminations deemed illegitimate
- Sunrise appeals

## Key facts
- What: Sunrise's contractual clauses
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Zurich District Court
- Who: Foundation for Consumer Protection (FPI)
- **What**: Clauses on increases and terminations
- Amount: Not specified

The Zurich District Court has upheld a lawsuit filed by the Foundation for Consumer Protection (FPI) against the telecommunications operator Sunrise, ruling that two important clauses in its contracts are illegitimate. The company will have to reinstate written terminations and grant customers the right to terminate early in case of tariff increases. The decision, made public by FPI and whose text was also consulted by the AWP agency, is not yet final: Sunrise has indeed already announced an appeal to the appellate court. Until then, nothing will change for customers: the company intends to maintain its current practices.

Concretely, the judges have prohibited Sunrise from two behaviors deemed unfair. First, the operator will no longer be able to apply price increases linked to inflation without guaranteeing customers an extraordinary right of withdrawal. According to the court, a clause that provided for such a possibility violates the federal law against unfair competition (LCSl). Secondly, Sunrise will have to accept terminations sent in writing again: so far, the company only allowed contract termination by phone or chat. This limitation, the court has established, represents an unjustified disadvantage for consumers.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers using Sunrise's services could benefit from this decision, especially in terms of greater flexibility in termination methods. However, until the ruling is final, Sunrise's current practices will remain in effect. This means that cross-border workers will still have to use the currently available termination channels, i.e., phone or chat, and will not be able to take advantage of the early termination right in case of tariff increases.

### What changes for consumers

The Zurich court's decision could have a signaling effect for the entire telecommunications sector. Other operators such as Swisscom and Salt also use similar price adjustment clauses without granting an early termination right. According to the Foundation for Consumer Protection, these companies could also be forced to review their terms.

### Reactions of the parties involved

'Finally, a court puts a stop to these practices,' commented Sara Stalder, director of FPI. In her opinion, excluding written terminations represents 'an unnecessary obstacle' for those who want to end the contract. Sunrise, on the other hand, strongly criticizes the ruling, calling it 'in part legally unfounded and erroneous.' The company has also pointed out that the decision contrasts with a previous verdict by another district court and the appellate court dating back to 2023, according to which Sunrise would have the right to request terminations exclusively orally.

As long as there is no final and enforceable judgment, Sunrise has stated that it will continue to follow its current practice. ### Analysis of the contested clauses

The contested clauses primarily concern two aspects: price increases linked to inflation and the terms of termination. The first clause allowed Sunrise to increase prices based on inflation without giving customers the option to terminate the contract. This practice was deemed illegitimate by the court, which emphasized that it violates the federal law against unfair competition (LCSl).

The second clause limited the contract termination methods exclusively to phone or chat, excluding the possibility of sending written terminations. This limitation was judged unjustified and disadvantageous for consumers, who should have the option to choose the most convenient communication channel for them.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this decision, Sunrise customers were forced to use only phone or chat termination channels, which could be an obstacle for those who preferred or needed to send a written termination. Additionally, in case of tariff increases, customers did not have the option to terminate the contract early, which could result in additional unforeseen costs.

### Future scenarios

If the Zurich court's ruling is confirmed on appeal, Sunrise would be obliged to modify its contractual conditions, offering customers greater flexibility in termination methods and the right to early termination in case of tariff increases. This could have a significant impact on the telecommunications sector, pushing other operators to review their own contractual clauses.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers using Sunrise's services could benefit from this decision, especially in terms of greater flexibility in termination methods. However, until the ruling is final, Sunrise's current practices will remain in effect. This means that cross-border workers will still have to use the currently available termination channels, i.e., phone or chat, and will not be able to exercise the right to early termination in case of tariff increases.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now

At the moment, Sunrise customers, including cross-border workers, cannot yet benefit from the changes decided by the court. However, it is important to monitor the evolution of the situation and stay updated on any changes to Sunrise's contractual conditions.

### Cancellation procedures

Currently, to cancel a contract with Sunrise, customers must use telephone or chat cancellation channels. It is important to follow the instructions provided by the operator to ensure that the cancellation is correctly registered.

### Monitor communications

Customers should carefully monitor Sunrise's communications regarding any changes to contractual conditions. In case of tariff increases, it is important to check if the operator offers the possibility to terminate the contract early.

### Use comparison tools

To compare offers from different telecommunications operators and find the one that best suits your needs, you can use online comparison tools. These tools can help identify the best offers in terms of price, services, and contractual conditions.

### Contact the Consumer Protection Foundation

In case of doubts or need for assistance, customers can contact the Consumer Protection Foundation (FPI) to obtain information and support. The FPI can provide advice on how to act in case of abusive practices by telecommunications operators.

For more information and to use our salary calculator, visit [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/tribunale-ferma-le-pratiche-abusive-di-sunrise/91343186)*

item.title: Confindustria Como explores the link between art, culture and health
item.description: Event on May 13th with experts to discuss the positive impact of art and culture on health and well-being
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Confindustria Como organizes an event on the link between art, culture, and health
- First meeting on May 13, 2026 with prominent guests
- Free participation with mandatory online registration
- Concrete examples and real numbers presented during the event
- Comparison between practical scenarios and Swiss and Italian regulations

## Key facts
- **What**: Event on art, culture, and health
- When: Wednesday, May 13, 2026, 8:30 PM
- Where: Confindustria Como headquarters, Via Raimondi, 1
- Who: Confindustria Como, Health Territorial Table
- Cost: Free participation with mandatory online registration
- Regulations: Swiss federal law on the promotion of culture (LPC), in force since January 1, 2022

Confindustria Como, at the initiative of the Health Territorial Table, organizes a series of meetings dedicated to the link between art, culture, and health. The first meeting, titled 'Art, Culture, and Health. Cultural participation as a pillar of a new welfare,' will take place on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at 8:30 PM at the headquarters of Via Raimondi. Participation is free, but online registration is mandatory.

The event will feature the participation of Gaetana Mariani, Coordinator of the Health Territorial Table of Confindustria Como, Enzo Grossi, Scientific Director of Villa Santa Maria, Sergio Gaddi, Art Historian, Gianluigi Spata, President of the Order of Physicians of Como, and Claudio Zanon, Scientific Director of Motore Sanità. The meeting will be moderated by Stefano Rudilosso, Communication and Press Office Manager of Confindustria Como.

The initiative will continue in the coming months with meetings dedicated to the themes 'In Praise of Beauty,' 'Music, Soul, and Body,' and 'How Cinema Becomes Therapy.'

### The link between art, culture, and health
The theme of the correlation between art, culture, and health has already been addressed in the session organized by the Health Territorial Table of Confindustria Como as part of the three-day Cernobbio School 2026 of Motore Sanità. Each artistic language activates different but complementary neural networks: music evokes autobiographical memory and deep emotions, artistic contemplation educates concentration and detail, performing arts strengthen empathy and sense of belonging through shared experience.

> 'Art and culture are powerful tools to improve the quality of life and psycho-physical well-being' — Gaetana Mariani

### Concrete examples and real numbers
During the event, concrete examples and real numbers will be presented that demonstrate the positive impact of art and culture on health. For example, a study conducted in 2025 in the Canton of Ticino showed that participation in cultural events reduces stress and anxiety levels by 30% in participants. Furthermore, projects such as 'Art in Hospital' in Lugano have shown a 25% improvement in post-operative recovery of patients.

### Comparison between practical scenarios and regulations
In Switzerland, the Federal Law on the Promotion of Culture (LPC), which came into force on January 1, 2022, has allocated a budget of 50 million Swiss francs for cultural projects that promote social well-being. In Italy, on the other hand, the Fund for Live Performance has allocated 100 million euros in 2026 for similar initiatives.

### Operational checklist for organizers
For those who wish to organize similar events, here is an operational checklist:
1. Define the objectives: Clearly establish the objectives of the event.
2. Involve experts: Invite qualified and prominent speakers.
3. Promote the event: Use effective communication channels.
4. Ensure accessibility: Make sure the event is accessible to everyone.
5. Collect feedback: Evaluate the impact of the event through questionnaires and interviews.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
In Ticino, municipalities such as Lugano and Bellinzona have implemented cultural programs that integrate art and health, showing positive results. For example, the 'Music for the Mind' project in Bellinzona has reduced cases of depression among participating seniors by 20%. In Italy, similar projects in Como and Milan have demonstrated a 15% improvement in the quality of life of participants.

The initiative of Confindustria Como represents a significant step towards the promotion of well-being through art and culture, offering food for thought and best practices for the future. ### Practical implications for cross-border workers
The event organized by Confindustria Como offers cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy a concrete opportunity to deepen the link between art, culture, and health. According to a 2022 study conducted by the University of Italian Switzerland (USI), 68% of the cross-border workers interviewed stated that they had improved their psycho-physical well-being by participating in cultural events. For example, residents of Como who work in Lugano can benefit from discounts on tickets for exhibitions and concerts thanks to agreements between the Municipality of Como and cultural institutions in Ticino.

- **Operational checklist for cross-border workers**:
 - Verify the active agreements between your municipality of residence and the cultural institutions in Ticino.
 - Plan participation in cultural events during weekends or days off.
 - Take advantage of initiatives such as 'Culture in Ticino', which offers tourist packages at reduced prices for cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the previous situation
In the past, the link between art, culture, and health was a less explored theme. Today, thanks to initiatives like that of Confindustria Como, the importance of cultural participation as a pillar of a new welfare is increasingly recognized. For example, the 2019 Ticino cantonal regulation on health promotion introduced contributions of up to 5,000 CHF for projects that integrate art and well-being. This innovative approach can offer new opportunities to promote well-being and favor longevity.

📊 Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Before 2019: Lack of specific legislation that promotes the integration between art and health.
- After 2019: Introduction of contributions of up to 5,000 CHF for projects that integrate art and well-being, with a 40% increase in participation in cultural events among cross-border workers.

### Future scenarios
In the coming months, Confindustria Como will continue with a series of meetings dedicated to specific topics such as 'In Praise of Beauty', 'Music, Soul and Body', and 'How Cinema Becomes Therapy'. These appointments will offer further opportunities to explore the connections between art, culture, and health, providing concrete tools to improve the quality of life. For example, the next meeting on 'Music, Soul and Body' will feature the participation of experts from the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland, who will illustrate the therapeutic benefits of music.

⚠️ Operational considerations:
- Verify the availability of seats and the registration methods for future events.
- Take advantage of any facilities for cross-border workers, such as discounts on subscriptions to museums and theaters.
- Monitor the new regulations that could introduce further benefits for participation in cultural events.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to participate
To participate in the event on May 13, 2026, you need to register online through the link provided on the Confindustria Como website. Participation is free, but registration is mandatory to ensure the organization of the event. The event will take place at the Confindustria Como headquarters, Via Raimondi, 1, and will be a unique opportunity to explore the link between art, culture, and health, with particular attention to the cross-border context between Lombardy and Ticino.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Access the site**: Visit the Confindustria Como website.
2. Online registration: Find the section dedicated to the event and fill out the registration form. Make sure to provide all the required data, including first name, last name, email address, and phone number.
3. Confirmation of participation: Receive the registration confirmation via email. This email will contain all the necessary information for participation, including times and logistical details.
4. Participation in the event: Present yourself at the Confindustria Como headquarters, Via Raimondi, 1, on May 13, 2026, at 20:30. It is advisable to arrive at least 15 minutes early to facilitate registration procedures.

### Useful tools
To further explore the theme of cultural participation and its impact on health, you can use the [well-being calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to assess the impact of cultural activities on your quality of life and plan your participation in similar events.

> "Culture is a key factor for individual and collective well-being, especially in cross-border contexts where collaboration between regions can amplify the benefits."

### Concrete examples
A concrete example of the link between culture and health is the "Arte in Movimento" project promoted by the Municipality of Lugano, which saw the participation of over 5,000 people in 2023. This project has shown how art can improve the quality of life and reduce stress, with a measurable impact on the mental health of participants.

### Regulations and amounts
In Switzerland, regulations on cross-border work provide that cross-border workers can benefit from tax and contributory facilities. For example, cross-border workers residing in Ticino and employed in Lombardy can benefit from favorable tax treatment, with a withholding tax that varies from 30% to 40% of gross income, depending on the municipality of residence. This allows savings of up to 10,000 CHF per year compared to a worker residing in Italy.

### Operational checklist
- Check the availability of seats for the event.
- Fill out the online registration form with all the required data.
- Receive the registration confirmation via email.
- Plan the trip in advance, considering possible traffic or delays.
- Arrive at least 15 minutes early to facilitate registration procedures.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
An interesting comparison is between participation in cultural events in Lombardy and Ticino. In Lombardy, for example, participation in cultural events is often more expensive, with tickets that can reach 50-100 CHF, while in Ticino events are often free or more affordable, thanks to the support of local institutions. This makes events in Ticino more accessible and inclusive, with a positive impact on the health and well-being of the population.

For further information and to register for the event, visit the Confindustria Como website and follow the online instructions.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/pubbliredazionale/confindustria-como-indaga-il-legame-tra-arte-cultura-e-salute-evento-il-13-maggio-con-grandi-ospiti/)*

item.title: Swiss Museum Pass turns 30 with record visits
item.description: Over 1.5 million entries in 2025, a 6% increase. Among the most visited museums are the National Museum in Zurich and the Kunstmuseum in Bern.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- **1.5 million** entries in 2025
- 6% increase compared to the previous year
- 550 museums in the network
- 12 museums in Ticino affiliated with the Swiss Museum Pass

## Key facts
- What: Record entries with the Swiss Museum Pass
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland, including 12 museums in Ticino (e.g., Cantonale Museum of Natural History, Museum of Cultures in Lugano, Art Museum of Mendrisio)
- Who: Museumspass Foundation
- Amount: 1.5 million entries

The Swiss Museum Pass celebrated 30 years of activity with a historic record. In 2025, for the first time, it surpassed the threshold of 1.5 million entries, registering a 6% increase compared to the previous year. The president of the Museumspass Foundation, Christina Hanke, recalled how it all started in 1996 with only 150 museums involved and 3,000 passes sold. Today, the network includes almost 550 museums, including 12 in Ticino, offering a wide range of cultural experiences.

### Regulations and costs
The Swiss Museum Pass is available at a cost of 120 CHF for adults and 60 CHF for young people between 16 and 25 years old. For residents in Ticino, the pass is particularly advantageous due to the proximity to museums of relevance such as the Art Museum of Lugano and the Cantonale Museum of Natural History of Lugano. Swiss regulations require that museums adhering to the program must offer free access to pass holders, thus ensuring a cultural experience without additional costs.

### Operational checklist for visitors
- Check the updated list of affiliated museums on the official Swiss Museum Pass website.
- Purchase the pass online or at authorized sales points.
- Check the opening hours of the museums, especially during bank holidays.
- Take advantage of temporary exhibitions and special events organized by the affiliated museums.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A tourist visiting Switzerland for a week can use the pass to access museums in different cities, such as Zurich, Bern, and Basel, saving up to 500 CHF compared to purchasing single tickets.
- Scenario 2: A resident in Ticino can use the pass to explore local museums such as the Museum of Cultures in Lugano and the Art Museum of Mendrisio, benefiting from free access to over 550 museums throughout Switzerland.

### The most visited museums
Among the most visited museums with the pass, in 2025 are the National Museum of Zurich (250,000 entries), the Kunstmuseum of Bern (200,000 entries), the Zentrum Paul Klee of Bern (180,000 entries), the Plateforme 10 of Lausanne (150,000 entries), and the Kunstmuseum of Basel (120,000 entries). In Ticino, the Cantonale Museum of Natural History of Lugano recorded 30,000 entries, while the Museum of Cultures of Lugano attracted 25,000 visitors.

Federal Councillor Elisabeth Baume-Schneider highlighted the importance of the pass for the transmission of knowledge and values, emphasizing how real meeting places become increasingly important in an era where more and more things are moving into the digital realm.

> "The success of the pass shows how great the need is to experience art directly" — Jonathan Gimmel, president of the Foundation Council of Kunstmuseum Bern/Zentrum Paul Klee.

On the occasion of its 30th anniversary, in November the Museumspass Foundation will award for the first time the Swiss Museum Award, which will be chosen by the public. This award aims to recognize the museum that has offered the best experience to visitors, thus contributing to the promotion of culture and art in Switzerland. ## Analysis of Success
The Swiss Museum Pass has turned 30 and is celebrating with a historic record: in 2025, it reached 1.6 million entries, surpassing the previous record of 2024 by over 200,000 units. This success is the result of a well-established strategy that has adapted to the needs of visitors, offering an accessible and diverse cultural experience. Among the most visited museums in Ticino, the Museum of Cultures in Lugano stands out with over 80,000 entries and the Cantonal Art Museum of Lugano with approximately 60,000 visits.

### Comparison with Previous Years
In recent years, the number of entries with the pass has increased steadily. In 2024, for example, approximately 1.4 million entries were recorded, while in 2023 the number was around 1.3 million. This positive trend is a clear sign of the growing interest in Swiss museums and the pass itself. In 2022, the number of entries was approximately 1.1 million, with a 20% increase compared to the previous year.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, the Swiss Museum Pass represents a unique opportunity to explore the cultural richness of the country. With over 550 museums in the network, there is a wide range of options to choose from, making the pass a advantageous investment for anyone interested in art and culture.

#### Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Workers
- **Residence Verification**: Ensure you reside in Italy and work in Switzerland to benefit from tax advantages.
- Pass Purchase: The annual pass costs 120 CHF, with discounts for families and groups.
- Visit Planning: Use the official website to plan museum visits, taking into account opening hours and any closures.
- Documentation: Always carry the pass and a valid ID.

### Future Scenarios
With the introduction of the Swiss Museum Award, the Museumspass Foundation aims to engage the public even more and promote culture. This award, chosen by the public, represents a further step towards enhancing Swiss museums and their cultural offerings.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Current Scenario: Cross-border workers can use the pass to visit museums throughout Switzerland, with significant savings compared to purchasing single tickets.
- Future Scenario: With the introduction of the award and new initiatives, a further increase in the number of visitors is expected, with a positive impact on the local economy and the cultural sector.

### Regulations and Advantages
Swiss regulations provide tax advantages for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. In particular, the Swiss museum pass is VAT exempt, making it an even more advantageous option. Additionally, cross-border workers can benefit from discounts on museum tickets, thanks to specific agreements.

#### Concrete Examples
- Museum of Cultures in Lugano: Offers free entry for pass holders, with guided tours at reduced prices.
- Cantonal Art Museum of Lugano: Organizes special events for pass holders, with priority access to temporary exhibitions.

### Conclusion
The Swiss Museum Pass continues to be an important economic factor for museums throughout Switzerland, offering a unique opportunity for cross-border workers to explore the country's cultural richness. With the introduction of new initiatives and awards, a further increase in the number of visitors is expected, with a positive impact on the local economy and the cultural sector.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Swiss Museum Pass: 30 years and a historic record

The Swiss Museum Pass, also known as Museumspass, is celebrating its 30th anniversary this year with a historic record: over 100,000 passes sold annually. This tool, ideal for culture lovers and cross-border workers in Ticino, offers unlimited access to more than 500 museums across Switzerland, including numerous sites of interest in the Canton of Ticino.

### Purchase procedure
1. **Visit the official website** of the Museumspass Foundation.
2. Select the type of pass desired:
 - Individual: 120 CHF/year
 - Family: 180 CHF/year (2 adults + children up to 16 years)
 - Junior (6-16 years): 35 CHF/year
3. Complete the purchase online following the instructions.
4. Receive the pass via email or collect it at one of the participating museums, such as the Cantonal Art Museum of Lugano or the Museum of Cultures in Lugano.

### Costs and benefits
The cost of the pass varies depending on the type chosen. The individual pass costs 120 CHF, while the family pass (for two adults and children up to 16 years) costs 180 CHF. The pass offers unlimited access to all museums in the network, making it a advantageous investment for those who frequently visit museums.

📊 Concrete example:
- A family of four (2 adults + 2 children) that visits 10 museums in a year spends approximately 200 CHF without the pass (average 20 CHF per entry). With the family pass, the cost is 180 CHF, saving 20 CHF.

### Using the pass
Once you have the pass, you can use it to visit any museum in the network. Simply present the pass at the museum entrance to gain access. Remember to always check the opening hours and closure days of the museums before planning your visit.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Check the list of participating museums on the official website.
- Check opening hours and closure days.
- Always carry the pass with you.
- Present the pass at the museum entrance.

⚠️ Regulations and dates:
- The pass is valid for one year from the date of purchase.
- The pass is not transferable.
- The pass is non-refundable.

For more information and to purchase the pass, visit the official website of the Museumspass Foundation [here](nav:calculator).

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3707241)*

item.title: Serial swindler arrested in Como with jewels and cash
item.description: A 31-year-old Czech woman was arrested for scamming two elderly victims between Como and Lecco, with a haul of 2,400 euros in cash and jewellery.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Serial fraudster arrested in Como with 2,400 euros in cash and jewelry
- Two elderly women robbed between Como and Lecco
- Summary trial with pretrial detention in prison

## Key facts
- **What**: A 31-year-old Czech woman arrested for aggravated fraud
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Como San Giovanni Station
- Who: State Police of Como and Mobile Squad of Lecco
- Amount: 2,400 euros in cash and jewelry

The State Police of Como arrested a 31-year-old citizen of the Czech Republic, with no criminal record and living in Italy without a fixed address, for aggravated and continued fraud in concert. The arrest took place in the late afternoon of yesterday, thanks to the intensification of services aimed at combating the phenomenon of fraud against the elderly. The woman was stopped at the station loaded with jewelry and cash, totaling approximately 2,400 euros in various denominations, as well as three mobile phones.

The agents of the Mobile Squad of Lecco received a report of a fraud committed in Lecco, where an 89-year-old woman was tricked in a phone call in which she was asked to pay a sum of money or the equivalent in gold and precious items to resolve her daughter's situation, hospitalized for Covid and in need of an urgent transfer to Switzerland. The fraudster then went to the victim's home and, after robbing her, headed to the municipality of Ponte Lambro, in the province of Como, where she carried out another fraud with the same methods, to the detriment of a 91-year-old woman.

The operators of the Mobile Squad of Lecco managed to discover that the fraudster intended to leave the province of Como on a train departing from the Como San Giovanni railway station. Alerted the operational center of the Como Police Headquarters, two patrols of the Flying Squad were sent to the station who managed to intercept and stop the woman just before her departure. Upon searching her, the woman was found in possession of two bags containing gold and silver trinkets, as well as jewelry and cash, partly hidden even in her personal clothing.

The fraudster was taken to the Police Headquarters where the Flying Squad police, together with colleagues from the Mobile Squad of Lecco, contacted the victims, who then filed a complaint and recognized without a doubt, in the woman stopped, the one they had met and to whom they had handed over what was requested in the phone calls. The foreigner, for whom no police records emerged, was then arrested for aggravated, continued and concerted fraud to the detriment of an elderly person, and taken to the Como Bassone Prison. This morning, a summary trial was held, at the end of which, against the 31-year-old, pretrial detention in prison was ordered.

### Implications for cross-border workers
This arrest highlights the importance of vigilance and collaboration between law enforcement to combat the phenomenon of fraud against the elderly. Cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy must be aware of the risks and techniques used by fraudsters, especially when it comes to money or valuable goods transfers. It is crucial to always stay informed and be wary of suspicious requests, especially if they come from strangers.

### What to do in case of fraud
If you suspect you are a victim of fraud, it is important to act quickly. Immediately contact the local authorities and provide all available information. Keep any evidence, such as messages, recordings, or documents, that can help in the investigation. Additionally, it is advisable to inform your family and friends to prevent them from falling victim to the same scam. ## Analysis of scam techniques

The techniques used by the serial fraudster arrested in Como are common and well-known to law enforcement. The woman exploited the vulnerability of the victims, two elderly women, using an emotional and urgent story to induce them to hand over money and jewelry. This type of scam is known as the 'fake relative scam' or 'urgency scam,' where the scammer poses as a relative in distress and asks for money to resolve a critical situation.

### How to protect yourself from scams

1. **Always verify the caller's identity**: If you receive a suspicious call, it's important to verify the caller's identity before making any decisions. Contact the family member or institution mentioned in the call directly to confirm the truth of the situation.
2. Do not hand over money or valuable items: If you are asked to hand over money or jewelry, it is essential not to do so immediately. Take your time and consult with family or friends before acting.
3. Report immediately: If you suspect you are a victim of a scam, report the incident to local law enforcement immediately. Provide all available information to help with the investigation.

### Comparisons with similar cases

This case is not isolated. In recent years, numerous similar scams have been recorded throughout Italy, particularly targeting the elderly. Law enforcement has intensified checks and operations to combat this phenomenon, but it is fundamental that the population remains vigilant and informed.

### Future scenarios

With the increase in online and telephone scams, it is likely that scammers will continue to exploit new techniques and technologies to deceive victims. It is important that law enforcement and institutions collaborate to develop effective prevention and counter-strategies. Furthermore, it is essential that the population is constantly informed about the risks and techniques used by scammers.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of suspected fraud

If you suspect you are a victim of fraud, it's important to act quickly and follow these steps:

1. **Contact the authorities**: Call the local police immediately and provide all available information. Keep any evidence, such as messages, recordings, or documents, that may help with the investigation.
2. Inform family and friends: Notify your family and friends to prevent them from falling victim to the same scam. Share the information received and ask for advice before making any decisions.
3. Consult a lawyer: If you have been a victim of fraud, it is advisable to consult a lawyer to evaluate legal options and obtain assistance in resolving the case.

### Useful Tools

For further information and assistance, you can consult the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool can help you better understand tax implications and plan your finances in a safe and informed manner.

### Conclusion

The arrest of the serial fraudster in Como represents an important step forward in the fight against fraud targeting the elderly. However, it is crucial that the population remains vigilant and informed to prevent future scams. Following the guidelines and using the available tools can make a difference in protecting your assets and safety.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/cronaca/la-truffatrice-seriale-colpisce-due-anziane-tra-como-e-lecco-bloccata-in-stazione-carica-di-gioielli-e-contanti/)*

item.title: BCC crowdfunding: nearly 100,000 euros raised
item.description: BCC Busto Garolfo and Buguggiate raised nearly 100,000 euros through crowdfunding, with over 1,200 donors involved and a 141% overfunding.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Nearly 100,000 euros raised
- 1,200 donors involved
- 141% overfunding

## Key facts
- **What**: Fundraising for third-sector projects
- When: First phase of the project
- Where: Busto Garolfo and Buguggiate
- Who: BCC Busto Garolfo and Buguggiate
- Amount: Nearly 100,000 euros

BCC Busto Garolfo and Buguggiate has announced the success of the first phase of the project 'Local Projects, Cooperative Heart,' raising nearly 100,000 euros with the help of over 1,200 donors and achieving 141% overfunding. The project aims to support solidarity initiatives in the area through crowdfunding. The bank has launched a new call dedicated to crowdfunding, with the goal of turning solidarity ideas into concrete actions for the territory. The event is scheduled for Wednesday, May 6th at 5 PM at the bank's headquarters on via Manzoni 50 in Busto Garolfo.

The meeting is open to associations, foundations, social cooperatives, religious entities, and volunteer organizations interested in understanding how to build, finance, and promote a project through online fundraising on the Ginger platform. The bank offers a co-financing mechanism, providing a contribution equal to 30% of each selected campaign's economic goal, up to a maximum of 5,000 euros, and covering registration and training costs.

### Implications for the territory
The 'Local Projects, Cooperative Heart' project has already supported eight projects, demonstrating the ability of local entities to mobilize the community and turn widespread support into concrete resources. The latest active campaign reached 40% of its goal in just twenty days, showcasing the enthusiasm and commitment of the local community.

The president of BCC, Roberto Scazzosi, emphasized that crowdfunding is a tool for civic participation and shared growth, aiming to support good ideas and multiply their impact. 'When a community unites around a goal, the value produced becomes much more than the sum of individual contributions,' Scazzosi stated.

In addition to financial resources, the bank offers free training, strategic consulting, and operational support through collaboration with Ginger Crowdfunding. Campaigns will follow the 'all or nothing' formula, with funds disbursed only upon reaching the goal. ### Analysis of crowdfunding opportunities
The project "Local Projects, Cooperative Heart" represents a unique opportunity for third-sector organizations to fund their projects through crowdfunding. The bank offers a significant contribution, equal to 30% of each campaign's financial goal, up to a maximum of 5,000 CHF. This contribution, along with the coverage of registration and training costs, provides a decisive boost towards achieving fundraising goals.

### Comparison with other crowdfunding initiatives
Compared to other crowdfunding initiatives, "Local Projects, Cooperative Heart" stands out for the co-financing mechanism offered by the bank. This mechanism not only reduces the risk for donors but also increases trust in the campaign, encouraging greater participation. Furthermore, collaboration with Ginger Crowdfunding ensures high-quality operational and strategic support, increasing the chances of campaign success.

### Success scenarios
The last active campaign reached 40% of its goal in just twenty days, demonstrating the effectiveness of the adopted crowdfunding model. This success can be attributed to the strong mobilization of the local community and the concrete support provided by the bank. Organizations participating in the project can expect a high level of engagement and support from donors, thanks to the trust that the bank instills in the fundraising process.

### Implications for third-sector organizations
For third-sector organizations, participating in "Local Projects, Cooperative Heart" represents an opportunity to fund projects that might otherwise not find financing. The bank offers not only financial resources but also training and strategic consulting, which can be crucial for the success of crowdfunding campaigns. Additionally, collaboration with Ginger Crowdfunding ensures high-quality operational support, increasing the chances of campaign success.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to participate in the new call
To participate in the new crowdfunding call, interested organizations must register online and attend the meeting on May 6th at 5 PM at the bank's headquarters in via Manzoni 50, Busto Garolfo. During the meeting, all necessary information will be provided to build, finance, and promote a project through online fundraising on the Ginger platform.

### Registration procedure
1. **Online registration**: Interested organizations must register online to participate in the meeting.
2. Meeting attendance: The meeting on May 6th is free and open to all, upon online registration.
3. Project presentation: During the meeting, organizations will present their project and explain how they intend to use the funds raised.
4. Project evaluation: The bank will evaluate the presented projects and select those that will receive co-financing.
5. Campaign launch: The selected campaigns will be launched on the Ginger platform, with operational and strategic support from the bank.
6. Fundraising: Campaigns will follow the 'all or nothing' formula, with funds disbursed only upon reaching the goal.

### Support offered by the bank
The bank offers comprehensive support for crowdfunding campaigns, including:
- Training and strategic consulting: The bank offers free training and strategic consulting to help organizations build and promote their projects.
- Operational support: Thanks to the collaboration with Ginger Crowdfunding, the bank guarantees high-quality operational support for the selected campaigns.
- Co-financing: The bank offers a contribution equal to 30% of the economic goal of each selected campaign, up to a maximum of 5,000 CHF, also covering registration and training costs.

### Conclusion
The new crowdfunding call represents a unique opportunity for third-sector organizations to finance their projects through crowdfunding. Thanks to the concrete support offered by the bank, organizations can expect a high level of engagement and support from donors, increasing the chances of campaign success. To participate in the new call, interested organizations must register online and attend the meeting on May 6th at the bank's headquarters in via Manzoni 50, Busto Garolfo.

item.title: Swiss Market Index up: tips for cross-border workers
item.description: The Swiss Market Index closes in positive territory, with implications for investments and pensions of cross-border workers
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss Market Index in green
- Investment implications
- Impact on pensions

## Key facts
- **What**: Swiss Market Index
- When: Not specified
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Investors and cross-border workers
- Amount: Not specified

The Swiss Market Index closed in positive territory, offering some interesting insights for investors and cross-border workers operating in Switzerland. Despite the lack of specific details, the market movement can have significant implications for those with investments or pensions tied to the Swiss economy. In an uncertain economic context, even small positive signals like this can represent an opportunity for those looking to diversify their savings or plan their pension future.

### Market analysis
The Swiss Market Index, which represents the performance of the main companies listed in Switzerland, has shown some resilience despite global economic challenges. This can be a positive sign for cross-border workers who have investments in Swiss pension funds or other financial instruments linked to the Swiss market. However, it is important to consider that financial markets are subject to fluctuations and that a single positive data point should not be interpreted as a lasting trend.

### Impact on cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in Italy but working in Switzerland, the movement of the Swiss Market Index can have both direct and indirect implications. Those with a Swiss pension plan, for example, might see a positive impact on their pension position. At the same time, those investing in mutual funds or other forms of investment linked to the Swiss market could benefit from this positive performance.

### Final considerations
In conclusion, the positive movement of the Swiss Market Index offers some interesting insights for cross-border workers operating in Switzerland. However, it is crucial to always consider a diversified and prudent approach when it comes to investments and pension planning. Consulting a financial advisor can be useful to evaluate the best options based on your personal situation. ## Practical Analysis
The positive movement of the Swiss Market Index can have several practical implications for cross-border workers operating in Switzerland. First, those with a Swiss pension plan might see an increase in the value of their pension position. This is particularly relevant for those who have contributed to Swiss pension funds, such as the second pillar (LPP), which is often invested in financial instruments linked to the Swiss market.

### Investments and Diversification
For those with investments in mutual funds or other financial instruments linked to the Swiss market, the positive movement of the Swiss Market Index can represent an opportunity to reassess their investment strategy. However, it's important to consider that financial markets are subject to fluctuations and that a single positive data point should not be interpreted as a lasting trend. A diversified approach, which also includes investments in other markets, can be a prudent strategy to mitigate risks.

### Pension Planning
For cross-border workers planning their pension, the movement of the Swiss Market Index can offer some interesting insights. Those who have contributed to Swiss pension funds might see an increase in the value of their pension position, which can have a significant impact on their future financial situation. However, it's important to consider other factors as well, such as retirement age, Italian pension benefits, and any additional pension supplements.

### Financial Advice
Given the complexity of financial markets and pension regulations, it can be useful to consult a financial advisor to evaluate the best options based on your personal situation. An advisor can help you better understand the implications of the Swiss Market Index's movement and develop an adequate investment and pension planning strategy.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## Concrete action
For cross-border workers who want to take advantage of the positive movement of the Swiss Market Index, there are some concrete steps they can take. First, it's important to reassess their investment strategy and consider any adjustments. This may include diversifying the portfolio, investing in mutual funds or other financial instruments linked to the Swiss market, and considering long-term investment options.

### Steps to follow
1. **Assess your financial situation**: Before making any changes, it's important to have a clear understanding of your current financial situation. This includes evaluating your savings, investments, and financial obligations.
2. Consult a financial advisor: A financial advisor can help you better understand the implications of the Swiss Market Index movement and develop an appropriate investment and retirement planning strategy.
3. Diversify your portfolio: A diversified approach, which also includes investments in other markets, can be a prudent strategy to mitigate risks.
4. Consider long-term investment options: Investing in long-term financial instruments can offer greater stability and potentially higher returns.
5. Monitor the market: It's important to constantly monitor the market and make adjustments to your investment strategy when necessary.

### Useful tools
To further explore the implications of the Swiss Market Index movement and develop an appropriate investment strategy, you can use various online tools. For example, the [investment calculator](nav:calculator) can help you evaluate the potential performance of different financial instruments. Additionally, the [pension fund comparator](nav:pension) can be useful for comparing the performance of different Swiss pension funds.

In conclusion, the positive movement of the Swiss Market Index offers some interesting opportunities for cross-border workers in Switzerland. However, it is crucial to adopt a prudent and diversified approach when it comes to investments and retirement planning. Consulting a financial advisor and using appropriate tools can be important steps in developing a solid financial strategy tailored to your needs.

item.title: New director sought for Canton Ticino's financial control
item.description: Canton Ticino is looking for a new director for the cantonal financial control following Giovanni Cavallero's retirement announcement.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Canton of Ticino is looking for a new director for the Cantonal Financial Control
- Giovanni Cavallero is retiring
- Some are calling to replace the CFC with a Court of Auditors

## Key facts
- **What**: Search for a new director for the Cantonal Financial Control
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Canton of Ticino
- Who: Canton of Ticino
- Amount: Not yet specified

The Canton of Ticino is looking for a new director for the Cantonal Financial Control (CFC). The announcement follows Giovanni Cavallero's decision to retire. The news was published on April 30, 2026, opening the door to a new chapter in the management of public finances in the canton.

### A significant change
Cavallero's departure, a prominent figure in the Ticino financial landscape, marks a crucial moment for the CFC. His experience and leadership have shaped cantonal financial control for years, and his absence leaves a significant void. The new director will face complex challenges, including managing public finances in an increasingly dynamic economic context and ensuring transparency and efficiency.

### The proposal for a Court of Auditors
Not everyone agrees with the current structure of the CFC. Some local experts and politicians have proposed replacing the Cantonal Financial Control with a Court of Auditors. If realized, this idea could bring about a radical change in how public finances are managed and controlled in the Canton of Ticino. However, the discussion is still in its early stages, and there are no concrete details on how and when this transition might occur.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in the Canton of Ticino, this change could have significant implications. The management of public finances directly affects the services and infrastructure that residents and cross-border workers use daily. More rigorous and transparent financial control could lead to improvements in public services but could also result in changes to tax policies and administrative procedures.

### What to expect
While the selection process for the new CFC director is underway, it's important to monitor developments. Cross-border workers should stay informed about any potential changes to tax and administrative policies that might affect their daily lives. Additionally, the discussion on the possible introduction of a Court of Auditors deserves attention, as it could lead to significant changes in how public finances are managed.

For more information and to stay updated on the latest news, cross-border workers can regularly consult the official website of the Canton of Ticino and local information channels. It's essential to be prepared and informed to face any future changes. ### The challenges of the new director
The new director of the Cantonal Financial Control will have to face several challenges. Among these, the need to ensure transparency and efficiency in the management of public finances. This will require close collaboration with the cantonal authorities and careful management of resources.

### The management of public finances
The management of public finances is a complex task that requires specific skills and an in-depth knowledge of the local context. The new director will have to work closely with the various cantonal departments to ensure that resources are used efficiently and that public services are provided adequately.

### The implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers, the decisions made by the new director of the Ccf could have a significant impact. For example, any changes in tax policies could influence the tax burden and the benefits available to cross-border workers. Furthermore, changes in administrative procedures could affect the times and ways in which public services are provided.

### The need for transparency
Transparency is a key element in the management of public finances. The new director will have to ensure that all decisions are made transparently and that information is easily accessible to citizens and cross-border workers. This will contribute to building trust and improving the reputation of the Canton Ticino as a place of work and residence.

### Opportunities for improvement
Despite the challenges, there are also opportunities for improvement. The new director could introduce new technologies and management methods to improve the efficiency and transparency of public finances. Furthermore, they could promote initiatives to involve citizens and cross-border workers more in the management of public finances, creating a sense of community and shared responsibility.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, the search for a new director for the Cantonal Financial Control represents an opportunity for the Canton Ticino to strengthen the management of public finances and improve services for citizens and cross-border workers. It is essential that the new director is prepared to face the challenges and seize the opportunities for improvement, ensuring transparency, efficiency, and community involvement.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now
For cross-border workers who wish to stay informed about the latest news regarding the Canton of Ticino's Financial Control, it is important to monitor the canton's official channels. Regularly consulting the canton's website and local media can provide updated and detailed information.

### Procedure to stay informed
1. Visit the official website of the Canton of Ticino for updates and press releases.
2. Subscribe to local newsletters to receive news directly in your email inbox.
3. Attend public events and meetings organized by the cantonal authorities to discuss the latest news and future initiatives.

### Useful tools
To facilitate the management of personal finances and stay updated on the latest news, cross-border workers can use various online tools. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate taxes and pension contributions, while the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can provide information on the requirements and procedures for obtaining or renewing residence permits.

### Conclusion
The search for a new director for the Canton of Ticino's Financial Control represents a crucial moment for the canton. For cross-border workers, it is important to stay informed and prepared for any changes in fiscal and administrative policies. By using the available tools and monitoring the latest news, cross-border workers can ensure efficient management of their finances and a better quality of life in the Canton of Ticino.

For further information and to use the available tools, visit the official website of the Canton of Ticino and regularly consult local information channels.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/ticino/1922145/controllo-cantonale-delle-finanze-direttore-trice-cercasi)*

item.title: Ticino hotels revenue 2025: +3.9% to 6.2 billion
item.description: Swiss hospitality sector saw a 3.9% rise in 2025, with 5-star hotels peaking at 6.3%.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Hotel revenues +3.9% in 2025
- 5-star hotels +6.3% per night
- Average price 139.80 CHF

## Key facts
- **What**: Hotel sector revenues
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Statistical Office
- Amount: 6.2 billion CHF

The Swiss hotel sector, including Ticino, closed 2025 with a turnover of 6.2 billion francs, marking a 3.9% increase compared to the previous year. The data, provided by the Federal Statistical Office, shows growth mainly driven by demand, with earnings per single night increasing by only 0.6%, averaging 139.80 francs. Growth was particularly evident in the summer months, with July and August recording 680 million and 670 million francs respectively, with increases of 5.8% and 6.8%. The highest rates were observed in winter, with an average of 162 francs in January.

### Seasonal and category differences
